description: THE RUSSIAN REVOLUTION Is an Uprising of the Workers Against All Oppression and All Exploitation of One Class by Another Whether in Germany Russia or the United States Peace Satisfactory to the Working Class of Germany Would Leave Wilhelm Where Nicholas Is Now Says Alexander Trachtenberg Of the 1905 Resolution it LEXANDER TRACHTENBERG the writer of this illuminating analysis of A A Russian revolutionary aims is an in structor at the Rand School of Social Science Mr Trachtenberg was a student in the Univer sity of Odessa at the outbreak of the Russo Japanese War He left the university to be come a soldier in the Russian army He was given the highest decorations for bravery but was later imprisoned uHth other students for revolutionary activities among the workers in the revolution of 1905 In this hazardous work he was in touch with Tchcheidze now president of the unique Council of Workmens and Soldiers Deputies and other leading figures in the present government Mr Trachtenberg sought asylum in America in 1907 entered and graduated from Trinity College and later re ceived a Masters degree from Yale He is now a candidate for a doctors degree in the latter university By
